senses.def_en: to sell in small quantities directly to consumers
senses.example: These shoes retail at $10. // the difficulties in retailing the new products
senses.example_zh: 这些鞋的零售价为每双 10 美元。// 零售新产品的困难

senses.example: a retail merchant
senses.example_zh: 零售商

senses.example: Many big department stores profit from buying wholesale and selling retail.
senses.example_zh: 许多大型百货商场整买零卖从中获利。

senses.example: small-tooth comb
senses.example_zh: 细齿梳

senses.example: He combed through the files searching for evidence of fraud.
senses.example_zh: 他详查档案,寻找欺诈行为的证据。

entries.mnemonic: 原材料在加工过程(course)中从粗糙(coarse)变精致

senses.example: His skin was coarse from years of working outdoors.
senses.example_zh: 由于常年在户外工作,他的皮肤很粗糙。

senses.example: A stand-up comedian is performing a coarse imitation of President Bush.
senses.example_zh: 一位滑稽说笑的喜剧演员正粗劣地模仿布什总统。

senses.example: coarse jokes
senses.example_zh: 粗俗的笑话

senses.example: Putting food into one's mouth with a knife is considered a vulgar act in England.
senses.example_zh: 按英国规矩,用餐刀将食物送入口中算是失仪。

senses.def_en: to tell again using fewer words
senses.example: I wrote a letter summarizing the main points we had discussed.
senses.example_zh: 我写了一封信,总结了我们讨论的要点。

derivatives: summary n. 摘要 a. 简明的

senses.example: The sum of 3 and 4 is 7.
senses.example_zh: 3 和 4 之和是 7。

senses.example: Bill wants to spend a large sum on modernizing the farm.
senses.example_zh: 比尔打算花一大笔钱使农场适应现代化的需要。

senses.example: be good at sums
senses.example_zh: 长于算术

senses.example: We should sum up the costs of production.
senses.example_zh: 我们应当计算出生产成本。

senses.example: Assembly workers were striking for all improvement in their overtime earnings.
senses.example_zh: 流水线上的工人们正为提高超时工作的工资而罢工。

senses.example: They pay £58 a day plus overtime.
senses.example_zh: 报酬为每天 58 英镑,外加加班费。

senses.example: There are a few people working overtime.
senses.example_zh: 有些人正在加班。

senses.example: There were murmurs of discontent from the workforce.
senses.example_zh: 工人不满,颇有微词。

derivatives: contented a. 满意的,知足的

entries.mnemonic: dis(not)+re(一再)+gard(留意)→不再留意→漠视

senses.def_en: to ignore or treat as unimportant
senses.example: He utterly disregarded my warnings and met with an accident.
senses.example_zh: 他全然不听我的警告,结果出了事故。

senses.example: She shows a total disregard for other people's feelings.
senses.example_zh: 她丝毫不顾及别人的感受。

senses.example: The room is in disorder. It's time to tidy it up.
senses.example_zh: 房间很乱,该收拾一下了。

senses.example: The prison abuse scandal caused social disorder in Iraq when it was revealed by press.
senses.example_zh: 虐俘丑闻被媒体披露后在伊拉克引起了社会骚乱。

senses.example: a disorder of the digestive system
senses.example_zh: 消化系统失调

extras.content: We should, therefore, learn to choose our words carefully and use them accurately, or they will make our speech or writing silly and vulgar. 译文:因此,我们应该学会审慎选词并准确地运用,否则就会使我们言词无聊而又粗俗难听。
